context: Tests whether the agent implements frame-drop persistence (~0.8s hold before no-face), garbage detection using the 0.85 distance threshold, and a diagnostic mode that logs distances to all enrolled people with correct interpretation thresholds.

checklist.name: No-face hold duration
checklist.description: shouldDeclareNoFace or the persistence constant uses ~800ms (0.8 seconds) as the hold time before declaring 'no face'

checklist.name: Persistence mechanism
checklist.description: shouldDeclareNoFace returns false (does not declare no-face) if the elapsed time since last detection is less than the hold duration

checklist.name: Garbage threshold 0.85
checklist.description: isGarbageDetection returns true for cosine distance > 0.85 (Haar false-positive threshold)

checklist.name: Garbage drops detection
checklist.description: Code or comments indicate that garbage detections (dist > 0.85) should be dropped entirely, not treated as 'unknown' faces

checklist.name: Diagnostic logs all enrolled
checklist.description: runDiagnosticMode logs the distance to every enrolled person, not just the closest match

checklist.name: Diagnostic distance format
checklist.description: Diagnostic output formats distances to 3 decimal places (e.g., %.3f or similar precision)

checklist.name: True identity threshold in diagnostics
checklist.description: Comments or diagnostic output interpretation mention that true identity distance should be < 0.45

checklist.name: Others threshold in diagnostics
checklist.description: Comments or diagnostic output interpretation mention that other enrolled people's distances should be > 0.55

checklist.name: Spread check in diagnostics
checklist.description: Comments or diagnostic interpretation mention checking that the spread between true match and next-closest is > 0.15

checklist.name: Distance semantics correct
checklist.description: isGarbageDetection and persistence logic treat lower distance as a better match (not higher), consistent with cosine distance semantics

checklist.name: Hold constant named clearly
checklist.description: The 0.8s hold duration is defined as a named constant (not a magic number inline)
